title: Nelumbium nelumbo
taxonomy: Nelumbium nelumbo is a genus of aquatic plants belonging to the family Nelumbonaceae. This plant is commonly known as the lotus. Nelumbium nelumbo is classified as a monocot, specifically belonging to the order Nymphaeales. Within this order, Nelumbium nelumbo is further classified into the family Nelumbonaceae. Nelumbium nelumbo is native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world.
uses: Nelumbium nelumbo, also known as the sacred lotus, has been used in traditional medicine for centuries in various cultures. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antibacterial properties, which may help in treating various health conditions. The rhizomes and seeds of the plant are used to create medicinal preparations, including teas and extracts. In traditional Chinese medicine, Nelumbium nelumbo is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's potential medicinal uses are still being researched, but its traditional applications suggest its value in promoting overall well-being.
benefits: Nelumbium nelumbo is known to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's extract has been shown to have antibacterial and antiviral effects, making it a potential natural remedy for various infections. Additionally, Nelumbium nelumbo has been traditionally used to treat fever, diarrhea, and other gastrointestinal issues. The plant's seeds and flowers are also rich in essential nutrients, including vitamins, minerals, and healthy fats. Consuming Nelumbium nelumbo in moderation may help support overall health and well-being.

parts: Nelumbium nelumbo, the medicinal parts of the plant Nelumbium nelumbo, also known as the sacred lotus, include its rhizomes, seeds, and flowers, which are used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. preparations: Nelumbium nelumbo is a plant used in traditional herbal medicine, and its rhizome is often prepared as a tea or tincture. side_effects: Nelumbium nelumbo seeds and flowers have been reported to be toxic, and consuming them can cause gastrointestinal symptoms such as nausea and vomiting. The plant's toxic compounds, including alkaloids and glycosides, can also lead to diarrhea, abdominal pain, and other gastrointestinal issues. In severe cases, ingesting large amounts of Nelumbium nelumbo can cause more serious health problems, including cardiac issues and respiratory failure.
